content.problemStatement:
Given an integer NNN, let FNF_NFNвЂ‹ be the set of factors of NNN. e.g., F6={1,2,3,6}F_6 = \{1,2,3,6\}F6вЂ‹={1,2,3,6}.

The radical of an integer NNN, denoted by rad(N)\text{rad}(N)rad(N), is defined as the product of the distinct prime factors of NNN. e.g., rad(12)=2Г—3=6\text{rad}(12) = 2 \times 3 = 6rad(12)=2Г—3=6.

Define an **antichain** of a set SSS of integers to be a subset of SSS such that for any two elements xxx and yyy in the antichain, rad(x)\text{rad}(x)rad(x) and rad(y)\text{rad}(y)rad(y) do not divide each other. e.g., antichains in F6F_6F6вЂ‹ are {}\{\}{}, {1}\{1\}{1}, {2}\{2\}{2}, {3}\{3\}{3}, {6}\{6\}{6} and {2,3}\{2,3\}{2,3}.

Given NNN, find the size of the largest antichain of FNF_NFNвЂ‹, and the number of antichains of FNF_NFNвЂ‹ of that size. Since the answers can be large, print both of them modulo (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7).

e.g., if N=6N = 6N=6, the largest antichain is of size 2, and there is only 1 antichain of that size.

Since NNN can be large, the input is the prime factorization of NNN. The input has two arrays of size MMM: **base** and **power**, and N=base1power1Г—base2power2Г—в‹ЇГ—baseMpowerMN = \text{base}_1^{\text{power}_1} \times \text{base}_2^{\text{power}_2} \times \dots \times \text{base}_M^{\text{power}_M}N=base1power1вЂ‹вЂ‹Г—base2power2вЂ‹вЂ‹Г—в‹ЇГ—baseMpowerMвЂ‹вЂ‹.


Input Format

The first line contains TTT, the number of test cases. Description of the TTT test cases follows.
Each test case starts with a single integer MMM.
The next MMM lines each contain 2 integers separated by a space. The ithi^{th}ith line contains basei\text{base}_ibaseiвЂ‹ and poweri\text{power}_ipoweriвЂ‹.


Output Format

For each test case, output one line containing two space-separated integers, respectively the size of the largest antichain modulo (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7) and the number of antichains of that size, again modulo (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7).
constraints: basei\text{base}_ibaseiвЂ‹ is a prime number for all 1в‰¤iв‰¤M1 \leq i \leq M1в‰¤iв‰¤M
For any 1в‰¤iв‰ jв‰¤M1 \leq i \neq j \leq M1в‰¤iоЂ =jв‰¤M, baseiв‰ basej\text{base}_i \neq \text{base}_jbaseiвЂ‹оЂ =basejвЂ‹
**NOTE:** Input files can be large. Please use fast input methods (e.g. scanf in C/C++, BufferedReader in Java)

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1. The largest antichain size is 2, and there is only one of it {2,3}\{2,3\}{2,3}.
Example case 2. The largest antichain size is 2, and there are two of those: {2,3}\{2,3\}{2,3} and {4,3}\{4,3\}{4,3}.
